title: Exact age office and authority remain unresolved
source_observation: The text establishes queenly status and actions but not modern legal categories
risk: Production could invent constitutional powers or a complete biography
decision: Keep age office and custody authority provisional until textual and historical review
required_review: Classical Tamil; political history; costume; production

title: Rajamadevi is grieving mother and accountable political actor
source_observation: Her son dies and she performs harmful actions through royal access
risk: A tragic mother reading could excuse abuse while a villain reading erases grief
decision: Hold love grief responsibility and power together

title: Public protection cannot conceal private punishment
source_observation: She argues for Manimekalai's release before arranging harm
risk: The audience could miss the transformation of custody into control

title: Three coercive attempts remain distinct
source_observation: The queen uses drugging fabricated reputation and starvation in separate actions
risk: Combining them into one vague villain act weakens causality and accountability
decision: Show each mechanism separately with responsible review

title: Sexual-reputation violence is not eroticised
source_observation: The false accusation weaponises social reputation
risk: Visual treatment could reproduce the harm or centre the accusation
decision: Focus on fabrication power shame and survival rather than spectacle
rationale: Protects Manimekalai and maintains ethical representation

title: Starvation is an institutional order
source_observation: Servants are ordered not to feed Manimekalai
risk: Treating it as private anger hides systems and intermediaries
decision: Show rooms servants orders and silence while retaining queen responsibility
rationale: Connects hunger ethics and state power

title: Karma does not make Udayakumaran deserve death or excuse the queen
source_observation: Manimekalai explains previous births and actions
risk: The scene could become victim blame or legal defence
decision: Separate karmic causation from punishment and accountability
rationale: Preserves Buddhist framework without moral simplification

title: Apology does not erase accountability
risk: The film could demand that the harmed person immediately restore the relationship
decision: Permit recognition without forced emotional closure

title: Keeping Manimekalai after recognition remains ambiguous
source_observation: Queen refuses Chitrapathi's request and says Manimekalai should remain with her
risk: A benevolent reading could ignore continued possession
decision: Play protection and control together until free departure

title: Rajamadevi's mature act is relinquishing control
source_observation: Manimekalai later announces departure after Aravana's teaching
risk: A final embrace could turn transformation into adoption or ownership
decision: Show space made for Manimekalai's independent path
